122402.explanation: There is a risk that residents may decide in a referendum with incorrect information. Providing proper information beforehand is more important.

122405.explanation: Provided that the products sold have a direct relation to the regular business operations and do not generate significant additional traffic.

122406.explanation: Initially, subsidy schemes from the national government should be sufficient. However, in cases where these schemes are not accessible for certain residents, support from the municipality could be an option.

122409.explanation: If there is insufficient centralized housing elsewhere, employers should solve this by providing housing on their own premises.

122417.explanation: This is arranged in our region through Stichting Leergeld.

122418.explanation: To some extent. This form of communication should be maintained as long as there is support among residents.

122420.explanation: That is true and must remain so. Musical education is, was, and will remain important.

122423.explanation: Safety is becoming an increasingly important part of our community. To maintain or strengthen residents' sense of safety, enforcement on the streets is necessary.

122424.explanation: Since safety is an increasingly important issue, enforcement in every form is desirable. Camera surveillance is a very useful tool for this.

122426.explanation: Since the market largely determines house prices, it is useful to continue exploring ways to financially support starters.

122427.explanation: Given the tight housing market, this is a plus.

122429.explanation: Rules are to be obeyed. These homes should only be used for temporary residence (sometimes in urgent cases).

122430.explanation: We must continue looking for good locations for housing developments. Primarily through infill but we must keep considering all possible alternatives.

122432.explanation: Among the group of residents who have demonstrable valid reasons regarding the affordability of municipal taxes, the right support must be provided through tailored solutions.

122433.explanation: It must remain an accessible way to obtain help.

122435.explanation: Electric driving increasingly has the future. We must not fall behind in facilitating charging points.

122441.explanation: Tourists also make use of the municipal facilities.

122446.explanation: This is already the policy within our municipality. We are already leading in the energy transition compared to surrounding municipalities.

122449.explanation: Financially less capable residents must be taken into account.

122451.explanation: We should not start this in our “smaller” municipality. It will certainly not promote livability and safety.

122454.explanation: To enable as many home seekers as possible to inhabit a home, we must think and act creatively. However, with good and strict rules and boundaries.

122455.explanation: Work is certainly being done on this. Places are becoming scarce but we must handle the space smartly.

122456.explanation: There are sufficient designated functions within our municipality that are adequate for this.

122457.explanation: We should leave that to the associations themselves.

122459.explanation: The contribution should be proportional to the benefit we have from it.

122402.explanation: We strive to have a village council in every village. Residents can express their important opinions here.

122405.explanation: No, possibilities must be considered per location.

122406.explanation: This is up to the residents themselves.

122417.explanation: Tailored solutions will need to be made for this.

122420.explanation: This is up to the parents.

122423.explanation: Too little police presence on the streets, more community enforcement officers can strengthen the feeling of safety. Note, do not create a police state.

122424.explanation: At critical points, in places where safety could be at risk. Privacy safeguards are necessary.

122426.explanation: In this way, our young people have a better chance of getting a home within our municipality.

122427.explanation: The need within our municipality is high and waiting times are long. All within what is legally permitted.

122429.explanation: Holiday homes must remain holiday homes.

122430.explanation: If we are not allowed to build outside the existing village boundaries, we will not solve the housing shortages.

122431.explanation: We find it too far-reaching to set social work conditions for social assistance benefits.

122433.explanation: Regional agreements have been made about this and we do not want to go against them.

122435.explanation: A calculation is used for this showing where they should be located and where the demand for electricity is highest.

122441.explanation: Do not unnecessarily burden tourists additionally.

122446.explanation: Preference goes to roofs; a ban on agricultural land goes too far.

122449.explanation: It is up to the parents.

122451.explanation: Does not belong in our villages.

122456.explanation: We see no added value in that.

122457.explanation: This is up to the associations.

122402.explanation: Involving residents is valuable, but representative democracy remains the starting point. Not every topic is suitable for a referendum.

122405.explanation: Side activities at agricultural companies require tailored solutions and are not appropriate in all situations. The municipality must assess each location for spatial, traffic-related, and environmental responsibility.

122406.explanation: Generating sustainable energy is primarily the responsibility of residents and market parties themselves. The municipality's role lies in enabling and facilitating, not in providing structural financial support.

122409.explanation: They must ensure good housing, but this does not have to be on their own land.

122417.explanation: Participation in sports is important, but paying membership fees is not a structural task of the municipality. We believe support should be targeted and temporary, allowing room for personal responsibility and existing arrangements.

122418.explanation: Independent journalism is important, but additional structural funding is not a given.

122420.explanation: Music education contributes to the broad development, creativity, and social skills of children. By enabling this through subsidies, we ensure every child—regardless of background—has the chance to discover talents.

122423.explanation: Safety is not automatically improved by appointing more enforcement officers. We believe that deploying and working effectively with existing capacity, good cooperation with the police, and smart prevention are more important than expanding the number of enforcement officers.

122424.explanation: Camera surveillance is an invasive measure that does not automatically lead to more safety. We believe targeted deployment, good enforcement, and prevention are more important than a general expansion of cameras.

122426.explanation: Starter loans can further drive up housing prices and thus do not solve the problem. Moreover, we believe the municipality should focus on accelerating housing construction and creating enough affordable supply rather than providing extra loans.

122427.explanation: Priority based on place of residence hinders flow and can lead to unfair exclusion of other housing seekers. We believe social rental homes should be allocated fairly and transparently based on urgency and need, not postal code.

122429.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation, and allowing permanent residence leads to undesirable situations and enforcement problems. Moreover, this does not structurally solve the housing shortage; the focus should be on realizing suitable, regular homes.

122430.explanation: Housing development sometimes requires space outside current village boundaries to meet demand. However, we believe this must be done carefully, preserving landscape, livability, and the village character.

122431.explanation: Participation and activation are important, but this must be appropriate and feasible for people on social assistance. We believe in tailored solutions that take health, personal circumstances, and chances of real job exit into account.

122432.explanation: Waiver of fees can be important for those who really need it, but must also remain careful and affordable for the municipality. We believe this policy should be regularly evaluated for efficiency, fairness, and financial sustainability.

122433.explanation: Aid for poverty must remain focused on people who truly need it. Raising the income limit makes the policy less targeted and puts extra pressure on municipal finances.

122435.explanation: Designating regular parking spots as charging points can increase parking pressure. We believe charging facilities should be integrated smartly and purposefully, without reducing accessibility for other road users.

122441.explanation: According to our municipal policy; tourists also use local amenities and it is reasonable that they contribute. Tourist tax is reviewed and adjusted every three years.

122446.explanation: The energy transition requires a mix of solutions, in which solar energy on agricultural land can also play a role. We do believe this must be done carefully, balancing landscape, food production, and local support.

122449.explanation: Tailored support for regular swimming lessons. Support where needed, with a focus on regular swimming lessons.

122451.explanation: The establishment of a coffeeshop brings both opportunities and risks for livability and safety. We believe such a decision can only be made after careful consideration, with clear rules, enforcement, and local support.

122454.explanation: This involves both opportunities and risks: room rental can contribute to more living space but can also cause nuisance and pressure on neighborhoods. Therefore, we believe tailored approaches and clear rules are needed instead of a general ban.

122455.explanation: Already arranged in the new Center Plan. Good accessibility is essential for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors of the center. Sufficient parking spaces strengthen the local economy and keep the center lively and accessible.

122456.explanation: The municipality must remain critical of extra functions and associated costs. We believe sports clubs and existing organizations should keep control as much as possible, with support where needed instead of new municipal roles.

122457.explanation: If clubs themselves express a need for more collaboration, the municipality should facilitate this.

122459.explanation: Good accessibility is crucial; the municipality must facilitate. A motorway entrance and exit on the A67 serve a regional interest and are therefore primarily the responsibility of the province and the national government.

122402.explanation: It may be an advisory referendum; the council takes the final decision. Specifically not for all topics and only for matters the municipality can decide or advise on.

122405.explanation: When converting intensive businesses to recreation, the municipality must cooperate flexibly. That does not mean everything is allowed.

122406.explanation: We can financially and administratively support energy cooperatives at their founding; afterwards, they must operate independently and autonomously.

122409.explanation: The municipality must create space with permits to make this possible and enforce quality. Possibly through temporary permits at shared locations.

122417.explanation: Everyone must be able to participate in society; this ultimately leads to lower costs for the government. We see this as prevention. Membership fees should be kept to a reasonable level and capped.

122418.explanation: This is a legal task until 2027 and is not decided at the municipal level. From 2028, this is a national government task, also not decided by the municipality. We support this national policy and believe the municipality should not reserve extra funds for it.

122420.explanation: This is already the case and included in the current multi-year budget. So maintain the current arrangement.

122423.explanation: The municipality must deploy more green enforcement officers in the Kempen region.

122424.explanation: We do not want cameras in the nightlife area of Reusel; safety must be ensured by hospitality owners and, secondly, by police and enforcement officers.

122426.explanation: Starters are often now unable to live independently due to limited availability of affordable homes. To give them an extra helping hand, we support a starter loan.

122427.explanation: In Hulsel, Hooge, and Lage Mierde yes; in Reusel also for housing seekers from outside the municipality.

122429.explanation: This should be handled in a differentiated way to prevent criminalization.

122430.explanation: This is not necessary. There is enough space within village boundaries to meet the objectives.

122431.explanation: The municipality does not decide on that.

122432.explanation: You should help fellow citizens who are struggling and not put them in further trouble. This ultimately benefits the municipality and the community more. This is a sensible preventive measure.

122433.explanation: To enable a good and dignified life for everyone, the municipality must generously support people with financial difficulties. The income limit should be raised to 130% of the subsistence level.

122435.explanation: In proportion to the number/ratio of non-electric and electric vehicles. Especially in places where people have no private driveway to charge electrically.

122446.explanation: Solar parks should not be prohibited. With the latest insights, solar parks can actually promote biodiversity. In this way, the agricultural transition provides additional value for nature.

122449.explanation: Swimming is a basic skill in our country that is necessary to safely participate in society. School swimming ensures that everyone, regardless of income, learns to swim.

122451.explanation: The municipality should not take the initiative for this, but if an entrepreneur comes forward, we can cooperate. Various substances are used by youth in the municipality. It is better to have this safely regulated than to let everyone do as they please.

122454.explanation: Given the housing shortage, other forms of housing use should be encouraged rather than discouraged. This often allows young people to become independent more quickly.

122455.explanation: Sufficient parking spaces have been included in the Reusel center plan. To what extent the blue parking card will be required must be determined by research after the center is completed and cannot yet be said.

122456.explanation: A sports official encourages sports among youth and thus contributes to the social bonding of young people, which ultimately leads to fewer social problems.

122457.explanation: Yes, collaborate and thereby ensure that each village maintains a viable local sports park. Sports parks and clubs are important for social cohesion in the villages.

122459.explanation: First, investigate how the existing road network can be optimized and what impact the new N284 has on traffic flow.

122402.explanation: Residents and entrepreneurs form the heart of our municipality. The municipal government has the task to serve their interests and take their voices seriously. For important issues, there must be room for direct participation so that the government truly acts on behalf of the community.

122405.explanation: Together with BVNL, I stand for free entrepreneurship and the government should interfere as little as possible with the free market. Farmers have built our municipality and form the foundation of our community.

122406.explanation: If people want to generate their own energy, that is fine. If they want to do it together, that is also great. But it goes way too far to spend taxpayers' money on this.

122409.explanation: Employers in agriculture and horticulture must arrange the housing of seasonal workers on their own company grounds so that social rental homes remain available for residents who are genuinely looking for housing. This should also be possible without a permit.

122417.explanation: No. Sports are important for health and well-being, but membership of a sports club is not among the primary basic needs. People can also exercise easily and at low cost, for example in public spaces.

122418.explanation: The annual budget for the PC55 newsletter alone is €6,000; nowadays people prefer digital news sources, have (external) news sources like the Eindhovens Dagblad, and want to decide for themselves which news to read/watch. Tax money should not fund these kinds of hobbies.

122420.explanation: Tax money or municipal subsidies for music lessons do not belong to the core tasks of the municipality.

122423.explanation: As a border municipality, we must put extra focus on safety. More enforcement officers (BOAs), better collaboration with the police, and visible enforcement are necessary to combat nuisance and ensure the safety of our residents.

122424.explanation: Part of our safety, visibility, and ensuring good social cohesion includes the fact that just across the border over 400 asylum seekers are housed, with a different culture and background that sometimes clashes with our own; safety comes first! Privacy must, however, be guaranteed.

122426.explanation: 130 vacant homes, we keep building but the vacant homes remain empty. The housing market must be unlocked by cutting rules and lowering taxes. But the government may not use taxpayers' money to equalize.

122429.explanation: Less government means more housing. Property rights must be respected. If asylum seekers can be temporarily housed in bungalows or campsites, it is also possible for people to live on campsites.

122430.explanation: We must keep building but focus less on 'owner-occupied homes' because 130 are already vacant, and more on 'social rental homes' or 'rent-to-own'.

122431.explanation: A reciprocal contribution in exchange for free money is only logical. Everyone can contribute to social safety and cohesion in the municipality. At the same time, the municipality can offer suitable work that contributes to the development of the municipality and its residents and entrepreneurs.

122432.explanation: The poverty trap must be eliminated. It is demotivating to work when people on social assistance, including benefits and exemptions, are financially better off than workers.

122433.explanation: Middle incomes often fall outside the scope of schemes: often both partners work, fully contribute to household expenses, and sometimes end up with less than someone on social assistance; we should look at what people have left at the end of the month.

122435.explanation: It is questionable how many residents actually drive electric cars. The municipality can encourage gas stations to install charging points for electric vehicles, but those who want to drive electric are primarily responsible for arranging their own charging facilities.

122441.explanation: I think tourists should contribute to the facilities they use in Reusel-De Mierden. The revenue from the tourist tax can then be used to reduce the burdens on residents and entrepreneurs.

122446.explanation: Farmers have been self-reliant for years until the municipality interfered. Farmers should decide what to do with their land. However, without subsidies, there will be no solar panels on farmland. It is ridiculous to sacrifice agricultural land to solar panels.

122449.explanation: I believe school swimming does not belong to the core tasks of education. Parents can arrange this perfectly well themselves.

122451.explanation: Agreed! We are located near the Belgian border; the hospitality sector can benefit from this, and by having tourists, such as visitors from Belgium, pay for parking and road facilities, the revenues can be used to lower municipal taxes for residents and entrepreneurs.

122454.explanation: The government is the cause of the housing market crisis. Less government means more homes. Currently, 130 homes are vacant. The current policy continues building while splitting existing homes can create affordable units for all social classes.

122455.explanation: More parking spaces are good for Reusel and good for entrepreneurs' turnover. We should also build an underground parking facility/garage.

122456.explanation: I think schools and parents can handle this perfectly well themselves.

122457.explanation: Collective cooperation or merging between sports parks can increase self-reliance without requiring community money as financial contribution.

122459.explanation: Reusel-De Mierden should do absolutely nothing.

statements.title: Sports parks in Hulsel and De Mierden must cooperate more.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Reusel-De Mierden must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Reusel-De Mierden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: More municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) who focus on safety must be deployed in Reusel-De Mierden.

titleParts.text: In Reusel-De Mierden moeten meer gemeentelijke

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: More camera surveillance must be introduced in Reusel-De Mierden.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Reusel-De Mierden must reintroduce the sports officer.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: There must be more parking spaces in the center of Reusel.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: Reusel-De Mierden must contribute to a new on/off ramp on the A67.
